name: Urdu
register: Reverent literary contemporary Urdu in the tradition of the beloved Kitāb-i-Muqaddas (the classic Urdu Bible) and the widely-read Urdu Geo Version — dignified, gently Persianized, but warm and intelligible to ordinary believers; the diction Urdu-speaking Christians already pray in.
quote_convention: Double inverted commas (wāwēn) ”…“ read right-to-left; reported/direct speech is commonly introduced with the complementizer کہ (ki) and the vocative particle اے (ai) marks address.
poetry_convention: Hebrew poetic parallelism (Psalms, Proverbs, prophetic oracles) set as discrete right-aligned verse lines (miṣraʿ), one colon per line, matched couplets kept adjacent with the second line indented; no forced rhyme, the parallel sense carries the line.

glossary.note: Filial divine name; باپ ('father') is the exact word the Kitāb-i-Muqaddas uses in the Lord's Prayer, not خُدا — آسمانی ('heavenly') keeps the reverent height without going generic.

glossary.gloss: our Father (vocative: اے ہمارے باپ, 'O our Father')
glossary.note: Primary filial divine name, on the wording of the traditional Urdu Lord's Prayer (اے ہمارے باپ). NEVER revert to خُدا or خُداوند; باپ is warm and paternal, exactly the child-to-father register FHB requires.
Definite filial name; the bare باپ carries the definite divine sense in context (cf. بیٹا, 'the Son'), preserving the father–son correlation without an article Urdu does not use.
Filial; same باپ stem as the possessive forms so machine conformance holds; آسمانی marks reverent transcendence.

Anchored to اردو ریوائزڈ (Rom 8:15 / Mk 14:36) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "ابّا، اے باپ". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
REVERSION TOKEN — the traditional generic word for God in Urdu Christian usage. Non-filial contrast term; use ONLY where the English keeps 'God' as God-as-God, never as a stand-in for باپ.

REVERSION TOKEN — the covenant-name rendering of the Urdu Bible (Yahweh). Non-filial. Note: 'Lord' as a title for Jesus is also خُداوند (e.g. خُداوند یسوع); disambiguate by context. Never a substitute for باپ.
Standard, revered Urdu Bible term (Arabic-derived); universally recognized.

Anchored to اردو ریوائزڈ (Mt 10:20) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "ہمارے باپ کی رُوح". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
The fixed Urdu Bible Christological self-title; instantly recognized, do not literalize with بیٹا.
The warm, filial correlate of باپ, and the word the Urdu Bible uses in John 3:16 (اِکلوتا بیٹا); keeps the father–son bond in the same word-family.
Traditional Christological title. Filial alternative باپ کا بیٹا ('the Father's Son') is available and coheres with the FHB vision — native reviewer to confirm which to adopt.
The exact John 3:16 wording of the Urdu Bible (اپنا اِکلوتا بیٹا); اِکلوتا conveys 'one and only' with covenant tenderness.
FHB renders 'kingdom of God' filially: بادشاہی is the standard Urdu Bible word for 'kingdom', re-anchored to باپ instead of خُدا.
Standard Urdu Bible spelling.
Standard; commonly یسوع مسیح when paired with the name.

Urdu uses the same word مسیح for both 'Christ' and 'Messiah'; where the English distinguishes, context and capitalization-equivalents carry it — reviewer to confirm no separate transliteration (مسیّا) is wanted.
Standard Urdu Bible term; نیا عہد = 'new covenant'.

For Hebrew ḥesed (loyal covenant love). اٹل ('immovable') foregrounds covenant loyalty. Traditional Urdu renders ḥesed as شفقت ('lovingkindness'); وفادار محبت ('faithful love') is a close alternative — reviewer to confirm.

For racham / splanchnizomai (gut-level tenderness). رحم shares the Semitic r-ḥ-m root ('womb') and is the Urdu Bible's word for being 'moved with compassion' (اُس نے رحم کھایا); distinct from اٹل محبت above.
The established Urdu Bible abstract noun (راستباز = 'righteous one'); no substitution needed.
The exact John 3:16 phrasing of the Urdu Bible; ابدی زندگی ('eternal life') is a more formal synonym if a loftier register is wanted.

glossary.gloss: to be born anew / from a fresh start
glossary.note: The Urdu Bible wording of John 3:3,7 (نئے سرے سے پیدا نہ ہو); natural and unmistakable to Urdu readers.
